content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: Non‑Disclosure Agreement (Two‑Way) – University of Bristol
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_15_nda_11

open std.strings

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – Parties to the agreement
type Party = {
    name: string,
    address: string
}

@ Clause 2‑3 – Information that is subject to the NDA
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    description: string,                     # free‑text description of the material
    marked: bool,                             # “Confidential” label applied
    source: Party,                            # the disclosing party
    is_public_domain: bool,                   # true if already public at disclosure
    is_independently_developed: bool,         # true if recipient already possessed it
    is_made_public_by_disclosing_party: bool, # true if disclosing party later publishes it
    is_received_from_third_without_restriction: bool,
    is_required_by_law: bool,
    is_copied: bool,                          # true if a copy/abstract has been made
    is_disclosed_to_third: bool,              # true if disclosed to a third party
    is_used_for_commercial_purpose: bool,     # true if used commercially
    is_reverse_engineered: bool,              # true if reverse‑engineered
    source_acknowledged: bool                 # true if the source is identified as “Confidential”
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – the concrete objects that appear in the agreement
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – the two contracting parties
entity disclosing_party: Party
entity receiving_party: Party

@ Clause 2‑3 – the confidential material that may be exchanged
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper Functions (pure, no side‑effects)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
func is_exception(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    info.is_public_domain or
    info.is_independently_developed or
    info.is_made_public_by_disclosing_party or
    info.is_received_from_third_without_restriction or
    info.is_required_by_law

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Obligations (O) – what the receiving party must do
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 3‑a – take proper and reasonable measures
rule O take_proper_measures:
    not is_exception(confidential_info) implies true always

@ Clause 3‑f – acknowledge the source and mark the material
rule O acknowledge_and_mark:
    (confidential_info.source_acknowledged and confidential_info.marked) always

@ Clause 5 – confidentiality period of ten (10) years from the effective date
rule O confidentiality_period:
    true always   # the solver will treat the trace length as the 10‑year horizon

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prohibitions (F) – what the receiving party must NOT do
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 3‑b – commercial use without licence
rule F commercial_use:
    confidential_info.is_used_for_commercial_purpose always

@ Clause 3‑c – disclosure to third parties without written permission
rule F disclose_to_third_party:
    confidential_info.is_disclosed_to_third always

@ Clause 3‑e – make copies or abstracts without permission
rule F make_copy_or_abstract:
    confidential_info.is_copied always

@ Clause 3‑d – disclosure to employees beyond those needed (modelled as a prohibition
# on any disclosure that is not required for the purpose)
rule F disclose_to_unnecessary_employee:
    confidential_info.is_disclosed_to_third always   # same predicate captures any unauthorised disclosure

@ Clause Hypothesis – reverse engineering of objects embodying confidential information
rule F reverse_engineer:
    confidential_info.is_reverse_engineered always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Permissions (P) – situations where the above obligations do NOT apply
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 4 – statutory or public‑domain exceptions
rule P exception_allowed:
    is_exception(confidential_info) always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# End of model
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
